creator: emz@norma.perm.ru
text:
I got a FreeBSD with some NFS shares mounted from the network. I decided to change its IP but forgot to umount NFS shares.

When trying to 'umount /mountpoint-of-a-nfs-share' without -f I got mount hanged up infinitely.
After successful umount -f I got the inability to log in via ssh, hanged up midnight commander, and 'reboot', issued from local console also hanged up. (Probably some other side effects had place too).

How-To-Repeat: Repeat similar actions.
